source_pr.title: resource_control: update static calibrate cpu params
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #44012

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?
- cpu formula is ms, the previous calculation forgot to multiply by 1000

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test


### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: +// Use atomic.Value to avoid data race in the test.
failure_signature: +// Use atomic.Value to avoid data race in the test.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency races or unsynchronized shared state. Asynchronous state transitions need polling/Eventually-style checks. This PR stabilizes it by: +// Use atomic.Value to avoid data race in the test.
fix_pattern: +// Use atomic.Value to avoid data race in the test.

evidence: evidence: +// Use atomic.Value to avoid data race in the test.

PR title: resource_control: update static calibrate cpu params
